conversation.user: PMP certification exam in 5 weeks. I have the PMBOK Guide (756 pages) and Rita Mulcahy's prep book (550 pages). I'm a project manager with 5 years experience. I can do 1 hour on weekdays.

expectedAI.diagnosis: 5yr PM experience → skip known Rita sections, focus on agile/hybrid concepts (post-2023 PMP changes). PMBOK 7th ed principles-based → full 756p NOT needed, selective ~200p sufficient
expectedAI.strategy: Rita main (550p) + PMBOK selective (~200p, NOT full 756p) + weekly mock exam. Weekday: Rita 45min + notes 15min; Weekend: Rita 2hr + PMBOK 30min + mock 30min. Total: ~56.5hr vs ~55hr available.
expectedAI.honestAssessment: Rita 27.5hr + PMBOK selective 20hr + simulator 7hr = ~56.5hr vs 55hr available. Almost exactly fits — no buffer for slow weeks.

mustNotSay: you need to start from basics

mustIdentify: PMBOK 7th ed → selective read, not full 756p
diagnosis.mustReflectInPlan: PMBOK selective ~200p (not full 756p), Rita as primary

mustNotSay: you need to start from basics
